ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS RICCI CORNELL, CLARICE LACAU, CORZAC, INC., DBA JELLY'S, AND DANCE CAFE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL IS DENIED. THE COURT DID NOT LACK JURISDICTION TO ENTER THE ORDER DISMISSING THIS ACTION. JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL AND THUS THE ORDER IS NOT VOID BUT VOIDABLE. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ACT TO VOID THE ORDER BEFORE IT BECAME FINAL, THUS PREVENTING THIS COURT FROM GRANTING THE RELIEF REQUESTED BY PLAINTIFF. EVEN IF THE COURT COULD GRANT SUCH RELIEF, THERE IS NO GOOD CAUSE TO DO SO BECAUSE A PLAIN READING OF THE COMPLAINT SHOWS THAT THE REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS INCIDENTAL TO THE PRAYER FOR DAMAGES. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE RELIEF SOUGHT IS WARRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
(Renotice) Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal
Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFFS RICCI CORNELL, CLARICE LACAU, CORZAC, INC., DBA JELLY'S, A DANCE CAFE'S (RenoticeD) Motion To Set Aside Dismissal - The motion is off calendar because the motion was heard on June 26, 2012. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/CK)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal
Off Calendar Jun-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
G. Whitney Leigh, Esq., of Gonzalez & Leigh, Tel. 415.912.5950, appearing for plaintiffs and moving party.
David W. Ammons, Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3954, appearing for defendant and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS RICCI CORNELL, CLARICE LACAU, CORZAC, INC., DBA JELLY'S, AND DANCE CAFE'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal is Denied. The Court did not lack jurisdiction to enter the order dismissing this action. Judgment of dismissal and thus the order is not void but voidable. Plaintiff failed to act to void the order before it became final, thus preventing this Court from granting the relief requested by Plaintiff. Even if the Court could grant such relief, there is no good cause to do so because a plain reading of the Complaint shows that the request for injunctive relief is incidental to the prayer for damages. The court does not believe relief sought is warranted. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jun-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
G. Whitney Leigh, Esq., of Gonzalez & Leigh, Tel. 415.912.5950, appearing for plaintiffs and moving party.
David W. Ammons, Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3954, appearing for defendant and opposing party.

Neither party opposed the tentative ruling. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Attorneys Fees is Granted. Defendant is awarded $11,900 in fees. Defendant is entitled to fees as the prevailing party and the amount sought is reasonable. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
